lessons.title: Learn: Text Processing and Search
lessons.content_md:
# Lab 06: Text Processing and Search

This lab focuses on finding, filtering, transforming, and counting text from files and command output.

## Learning Objectives

- Use `grep` for pattern matching and filtering
- Use `cut`, `sort`, `uniq`, `tr`, and `wc` for text processing
- Combine tools with pipes (`|`) for fast analysis
- Use `find` + `xargs` patterns for scalable search tasks

## Core Topics

### 1) Pattern Matching with grep

```bash
grep 'error' app.log
grep -i 'warning' app.log
grep -n 'failed' auth.log
grep -r 'token' ./project
```

### 2) Column and Token Processing

```bash
cut -d':' -f1 /etc/passwd
tr ' ' '\n' < text.txt
```

### 3) Sorting and Deduplication

```bash
sort names.txt
sort names.txt | uniq
sort names.txt | uniq -c
```

### 4) Counting

```bash
wc -l file.txt
wc -w file.txt
wc -c file.txt
```

### 5) Search + Pipe Workflow

```bash
cat app.log | grep ERROR | wc -l
find . -name '*.log' | sort
```

Use these skills to investigate logs and extract useful insights quickly.

lessons.title: Command Practice Part 1 (10 Commands)
lessons.content_md:
## Guided Commands: Part 1

Prepare sample logs and practice filtering.

```bash
cd ~ && mkdir -p ~/lab6
printf 'INFO start\nERROR failed login\nWARN retry\nERROR db timeout\n' > ~/lab6/app.log
cat ~/lab6/app.log
grep 'ERROR' ~/lab6/app.log
grep -i 'warn' ~/lab6/app.log
grep -n 'ERROR' ~/lab6/app.log
wc -l ~/lab6/app.log
wc -w ~/lab6/app.log
cat ~/lab6/app.log | grep ERROR | wc -l
cat ~/lab6/app.log | grep INFO
```

tasks.description: C01: Create lab6 directory in home.
C02: Create app.log with INFO, ERROR, WARN lines.
tasks.hint:
Type: printf 'INFO start
ERROR failed login
WARN retry
ERROR db timeout
' > ~/lab6/app.log && cat ~/lab6/app.log

tasks.description: C03: Print full app.log content.
C04: Filter only ERROR lines from app.log.
C05: Case-insensitive grep for warn.
C06: Show line numbers for ERROR lines.
C07: Count number of lines in app.log.
C08: Count number of words in app.log.
C09: Count ERROR lines using pipe workflow.
C10: Filter INFO line from app.log using pipe.

lessons.title: Command Practice Part 2 (10 Commands)
lessons.content_md:
## Guided Commands: Part 2

Now practice cut, sort, uniq, tr, find, and xargs.

```bash
printf 'ali\nrahim\nali\nsadia\nrahim\n' > ~/lab6/users.txt
cat ~/lab6/users.txt
sort ~/lab6/users.txt
sort ~/lab6/users.txt | uniq
sort ~/lab6/users.txt | uniq -c
cut -d':' -f1 /etc/passwd | head -n 8
echo 'alpha beta gamma' | tr ' ' '\n'
find ~/lab6 -name '*.txt'
find ~/lab6 -name '*.txt' | xargs wc -l
grep -r 'ali' ~/lab6
```

tasks.description: C11: Create users.txt with duplicate names.
tasks.hint:
Type: printf 'ali
rahim
ali
sadia
rahim
' > ~/lab6/users.txt && cat ~/lab6/users.txt

tasks.description: C12: Print users.txt file.
C13: Sort names from users.txt.
C14: Show unique sorted names.
C15: Show unique counts per name.
C16: Extract first field (usernames) from /etc/passwd.
C17: Convert spaces to new lines using tr.
C18: Find all txt files under lab6.
C19: Count lines for all txt files via xargs.
C20: Recursive grep for ali in lab6.

lessons.title: Exercises (5)
lessons.content_md:
## Practical Exercises

Use your search and text-processing skills to solve all five tasks.

- E1-E2: easy
- E3-E4: medium
- E5: medium-hard

tasks.description: E1: Create events.log with 5 lines including at least two ERROR entries.
tasks.hint:
Type: printf 'INFO boot
ERROR auth
WARN cache
ERROR db
INFO done
' > ~/lab6/events.log && cat ~/lab6/events.log

tasks.description: E2: Count how many ERROR lines are in events.log.
E3: Show events.log with line numbers for each line.
E4: Create names.txt with duplicates and output unique sorted names.
tasks.hint:
Type: printf 'zara
rahim
zara
' > ~/lab6/names.txt && sort ~/lab6/names.txt | uniq

tasks.description: E5: Find all .log files in lab6 and count total lines across them.

lessons.title: MCQ Quiz (5)
lessons.content_md:
## MCQ: Text Processing and Search

Answer using option index (0-3). Example: submit 2.

tasks.description: Q1: Which command searches text by pattern?
Q2: Which command removes adjacent duplicate lines after sorting?
Q3: Which command extracts columns using a delimiter?
Q4: In `cat file | grep ERROR`, what does `|` do?
tasks.validation_rule: ["Deletes file","Sorts output","Creates directory","Passes output to next command"]

tasks.description: Q5: Which command counts lines in a file?
